title: "How I Unwind" — Marble Sort Satisfaction UGC
hook_3s: "POV: the only thing that turns my brain off after a long day" — pastel marbles cascade onto a conveyor in buttery slow-mo.
scene_flow: 0–3s: Selfie-style UGC of a 30-something woman in a cozy pastel sweater on her couch, soft daylight, exhaling and smiling at her phone. Overlay hook teases the satisfaction.
3–7s: Over-the-shoulder cut to phone screen — finger taps a tray, marbles tumble onto the conveyor with a juicy physics bounce, sorting into matching colored boxes. ASMR clinks.
7–11s: Quick montage of 2–3 escalating levels: a near-miss save, a special rainbow marble, a perfect clear with a little confetti pop. Creator does a tiny satisfied shoulder-shimmy.
11–14s: Back to creator face, eyes-closed chef's kiss, mouths 'so satisfying.' Phone screen visible with a level-complete star burst.
14–15s: End card — Marble Sort! logo on lavender background with orange CTA button pulsing.
visual_direction: Bright, airy, daylight UGC — the OPPOSITE of the dim blue archetype. Lean fully into Marble Sort!'s lavender (#E5DFFF) and soft grey (#F0F0F8) world: pastel throw blanket, cream couch, a vase of dried flowers in the background. Gameplay capture is crisp and centered, marbles rendered in the clean 3D cartoon style with soft rounded shapes. Orange accent (#FFB13B) shows up only on text overlays, the CTA, and the in-game highlights so the eye tracks the call-to-action. Editing is calm — no jump-cuts, gentle whip transitions, ASMR-forward sound design (marble clinks, soft taps, a single lo-fi beat).
text_overlays: POV: how I unwind after work
no ads. no timers. just marbles.
rationale: Keeps the archetype's proven "satisfaction confession" UGC structure but corrects every fit issue flagged: female creator in the 25–55 sweet spot, daylight pastel mise-en-scène matching the game's calm brand, and a mandatory over-the-shoulder gameplay cutaway so the physics-tap sort loop is legible within 7 seconds. UA should test this against a no-gameplay control to isolate whether the cutaway lifts IPM, and produce 2–3 creator variants (cozy/desk/bed setting) for cheap iteration.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, 9:16 vertical aspect ratio, clean and colorful 3D cartoon style with soft, rounded shapes, over-the-shoulder shot of a woman's hand holding a phone, on-screen gameplay shows pastel marbles in mint, peach, and lavender tumbling from a tray onto a light grey conveyor belt and dropping into matching colored boxes, soft lavender room background (#E5DFFF), light grey machine surfaces (#F0F0F8), vibrant orange (#FFB13B) UI highlight on a level star, bold orange on-screen text overlay reading 'POV: how I unwind after work', soft daylight, shallow depth of field, cozy and satisfying mood

Storyboard frame, 9:16 vertical aspect ratio, clean and colorful 3D cartoon style with soft, rounded shapes, close-up macro of three glossy pastel marbles mid-bounce on a light grey (#F0F0F8) conveyor belt, lavender background (#E5DFFF), tiny motion-blur trails, a vibrant orange (#FFB13B) sorting box glowing in the foreground, chunky orange overlay text 'tap → drop → sort', ASMR product-shot lighting, ultra-satisfying

End card storyboard frame, 9:16 vertical aspect ratio, clean and colorful 3D cartoon style with soft, rounded shapes, centered Marble Sort! logo over a smooth lavender (#E5DFFF) gradient background with a few floating pastel marbles and soft grey (#F0F0F8) conveyor silhouette, large rounded orange (#FFB13B) CTA button with white text 'Play Free', minimal clean composition, calm and inviting
